summary: Rhododendron mucronulatum, the Korean rhododendron or Korean rosebay ( Korean: 진달래; RR: Jindalrae), is a rhododendron species native to Korea, Mongolia, Russia, and parts of northern China. It is a deciduous shrub that grows to 1–2 m (3.3–6.6 ft) in height, with elliptic or elliptic- lanceolate leaves, 3–7 cm ( 1 + 1 ⁄ 4 – 2 + 3 ⁄ 4 in) long by 1–3.5 cm ( 3 ⁄ 8 – 1 + 3 ⁄ 8 in) wide. The reddish-purple flowers appear in late winter or early spring, often on the bare branches before the foliage unfurls. It inhabits forested regions at 1,600–2,300 m (5,200–7,500 ft).
